narrative:
On July 13, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 42.8%, taking up 1,205 of the 2,816 scheduled cases across 48 judges. This performance reflects a slight decline in court activity compared to recent prior sessions, which stood at 45.4% on July 10 and 44.3% on July 09, though it remains higher than the 37.5% hearing rate observed on July 08.

Individual judge workloads and clearance rates varied significantly. M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 managed the highest volume of cases, hearing 123 out of 250 (49.2%) in his primary list and 62 of 140 (44.3%) in an additional list. High disposal efficiency was demonstrated by V. Srishananda in Hall 21, who completed hearings for 78 of 104 scheduled cases (75.0%), and R. Nataraj in Hall 12, who heard 72 of 102 cases (70.6%). Conversely, Vijaykumar A. Patil in Hall 32 did not proceed with any of his 78 scheduled cases.

Analysis of hearing stages reveals that Orders represented the largest share of the docket, with 453 of 1,054 cases heard (43.0%). Preliminary Hearings achieved a 51.9% conversion rate (138 of 266), while Final Hearings conversion lagged at 21.2% (98 of 463). 

Among major case types, Writ Petitions (WP) dominated the court's schedule with 1,178 listings, of which 447 were heard (37.9%). This indicates a drop in WP clearance compared to July 10, when the hearing rate was 47.5%. In contrast, Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) saw improved traction with 187 of 342 cases heard (54.7%), up from 51.0% on July 10. Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) also maintained steady disposal, converting 246 of 533 scheduled matters (46.2%).
